channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania boosts digital infrastructure with 758 new communication towers and fibre optic links
item.description: On 10th April 2026, President Samia Suluhu Hassan officially launched 758 new communication towers and upgraded fibre optic backbone connections across 85 districts at Jakaya Kikwete Convention Centre...

item.title: CSOs and Bille Community Call for Urgent Action Over Gas Eruption
item.description: Civil Society Organisations (CSOs) and the people of Bille Kingdom in Rivers State have raised alarm over a persistent underwater gas eruption in the community. They have demanded an urgent response f...

item.title: CCM reminds young Tanzanians to carry forward Nyerere’s legacy of national unity and Pan-African ideals
item.description: The Chama Cha Mapinduzi (CCM) party has appealed to Tanzania’s youth to safeguard and advance the principles of the nation’s founding father, Mwalimu Julius Nyerere. Speaking during the commemoration ...

item.title: Cameron Smith suggests Cameron Bukowski as temporary hooker for Broncos amid injuries
item.description: Cameron Smith, the veteran NRL player, has suggested that young Broncos talent Cameron Bukowski could step in as hooker for the club in light of a current injury crisis. With the Broncos struggling to...

item.title: Alexandra township faces severe police shortage amid high crime rates
item.description: In Alexandra, Johannesburg, one of the most crime-affected areas in South Africa, 305 police officers are responsible for a population of about one million residents. This alarming ratio of one office...

item.title: Derek Dixon’s Private Life: Marriage and Dating History Explained
item.description: Derek Dixon, an American actor best known for his role in the TV series 'The Oval', is not married, and little is known about his personal relationships. The actor has kept his dating history private,...

item.title: Army delays reporting missing soldier, family criticises two-week lapse
item.description: The parents of Lance Corporal Ryan Rudd, 28, have expressed outrage at the British Army after a two-week delay in recognising their son was missing, a gap they believe may have cost his life. Ryan was...

item.title: High Court Extends Orders Barring Government Interference in Nairobi Hospital Management
item.description: In a significant ruling delivered on 10th April 2026, the High Court in Kenya extended conservatory orders that prevent the government from interfering with the management of The Nairobi Hospital, one...

item.title: The Transformation of Modern Warfare Through Artificial Intelligence: A Study of the US-Iran Digital Conflict
item.description: The article by Raja Muhammad Atif Azad explores how artificial intelligence (AI) has shifted the US-Iran conflict from physical battlegrounds to digital and civilian spaces, where data centres, techno...

item.title: Analyzing the US-Iran Ceasefire: What Comes Next?
item.description: In this episode of 'In the Public Square', Rappler columnist John Nery is joined by Alvin P. Ang, an economics professor from Ateneo de Manila University, to discuss the precarious ceasefire between t...

item.title: A Historical Overview of U.S. Presidential and Vice Presidential Visits to Pakistan
item.description: The article provides a detailed account of past visits by U.S. presidents and vice presidents to Pakistan, tracing the pattern of diplomatic engagement between the two nations. It begins with the anti...

item.title: Malacañang refutes false online claims about Marcos’ health
item.description: Malacañang and Rappler have jointly debunked social media posts falsely alleging that President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has been diagnosed with stage 4 colon cancer. The misleading claim, first posted on...

item.title: South Africa Receives First Shipment of Lenacapavir for HIV Prevention
item.description: The United States has welcomed the arrival of the first shipment of Lenacapavir in South Africa on 7 April 2026, marking a major milestone in HIV prevention efforts. South Africa received 37,920 doses...

item.title: 416 Ngoshe residents held by Boko Haram appeal for government and international help
item.description: Over 400 people from Ngoshe community in Gwoza LGA, Borno State, were abducted by a faction of Boko Haram more than one month ago. A recent video released by the insurgents, identified as Jama’atu Ahl...

item.title: Two children die after being locked inside a car in Olongapo
item.description: Two young children tragically lost their lives after being accidentally locked inside a vehicle in Olongapo City, Zambales. The incident occurred during the intense summer heat, which made the inside ...

item.title: Pakistan Navy conducts humanitarian rescue of merchant vessel crew in Arabian Sea
item.description: In a prompt and well-coordinated maritime rescue, the Pakistan Navy successfully evacuated 18 crew members, including foreign nationals, from a merchant vessel named MV Gold Autumn in the North Arabia...

item.title: Understanding the Importance and Challenges of Bees in North America
item.description: Bee populations in North America are facing significant challenges, but managed honey bees are not the main cause of native bee declines. While some worry that honey bees outcompete native species, re...

item.title: German Neo-Nazi Detained in Czech Republic Following Incident with Transgender Person
item.description: Authorities in the Czech Republic have detained a German man affiliated with neo-Nazi groups following a confrontation involving a transgender individual. The incident, which has drawn international a...

item.title: DA leadership race heats up as Geordin Hill-Lewis and Sibusiso Dyonase battle for top spot
item.description: As the DA prepares for its Federal Congress in Midrand this weekend, two candidates are vying for the leadership position: Geordin Hill-Lewis, Mayor of Cape Town, and Sibusiso Dyonase, the party’s cau...

item.title: Xi Jinping meets Taiwan opposition leader, stresses cross-strait unity
item.description: On 10th April 2026, China’s President Xi Jinping welcomed Kuomintang (KMT) chairwoman Cheng Li-wun in Beijing, marking the first visit by a Taiwan opposition leader in a decade. Xi expressed strong co...
